headline: A code interpreter gives NO significant accuracy benefit on questions the no-code agent can answer (point estimate slightly NEGATIVE); its entire large pooled advantage is mechanical context-overflow avoidance on big patients. Architecture/data-plumbing, not compute.
grading: deterministic(clean numerics + failures) + 3-Claude-judge panel; cross-checked by an independent codex/GPT judge panel; numeric subset validated against non-LLM ground truth

matched_budget_both_answered.reading: no significant difference; slight negative point estimate when both arms actually answer

resource_real_predefined.broke_decomposition: 11 broke = 4 code errored/overflowed (reliability) + 7 wrong real answer (reasoning)
resource_real_predefined.reading: predefined by resource success; code significantly worse, ~1/3 driven by code's higher error rate

large_record_resource_overflow.reading: resource overflows 32k by construction; code routes payload to a sandbox — the only positive effect, and it is architectural

pooled.reading: ENTIRELY the overflow stratum; valid as a deployed-architecture comparison, NOT as a reasoning gain

judge_reliability.magnitude_finding: gpt-5-mini wrongly rejected 43-44 essentially-exact numeric answers (median rel-error 0.0) and wrongly accepted 0 gross misses — one-directional precision-punishing bias; this manufactured the earlier phantom 'code hurts'.

superseded_passes.note: Two earlier headlines were SUPERSEDED and are kept for the audit trail (TRUSTWORTHY_REGRADE.md): (1) gpt-5-mini naive '+11pp code wins' = context-overflow + harness-bug artifact; (2) a deterministic-numeric-only pass mis-graded the 115 boolean Yes/No golds as numeric, inflating a '+1.4pp matched-budget null'. The boolean bug is fixed here (build_labels.py routes 0/1 golds to the semantic panel).

reproduce: python build_labels.py && python final_grade.py ; python judge_leaderboard.py ; python magnitude_analysis.py ; bash codex_panel.sh && python codex_judge_compare.py
